title: Rework the on-goal execute-path tasks until the real task is high-quality complete

stories.title: Rework the exact controller-backed onboarding path instead of closing on local-only evidence
stories.scope: Rework the same single-device monitor/log onboarding path targeted by D2ON-017, starting from the current blocker recorded in D2ON-018. The backend/frontend contract must no longer stop at "local evidence was persisted". Instead, it must carry one exact controller-backed execution result or one exact stage blocker through the API/UI contract so the path is judged by the real task outcome rather than by a formal story footer.
nonGoals: Do not reopen browser-only validation as the main task.
Do not fabricate remote success when the controller-backed step did not actually run.
acceptance: The exact single-device onboarding path no longer closes on local-only evidence when the controller-backed execution result is still missing.
One real controller-backed execution result or one exact stage-specific blocker is surfaced through the onboarding action evidence for that path.

The API/UI contract explicitly exposes which stage was reached: controller request not attempted, controller request attempted but failed, controller returned remote blocker, or controller returned a real execution result.
Focused validation covers the changed backend/frontend execution path.
notes: Rework target: D2ON-017 stayed on goal but still did not reach high-quality completion of the real execution task.
Not enough for DONE: only persisting onboarding evidence, only improving copy, or only rephrasing blocker docs without exposing a controller-backed execution stage/result.

stories.title: Rework the real single-device execution probe until it yields a controller-backed trace or exact blocker
stories.scope: Re-run the same real single-device continue-onboarding execution concern targeted by D2ON-018, but require the result to be a controller-backed execution trace or an exact blocker produced by that path. A local-only evidence summary is not sufficient completion for this rework story. The evidence must name the exact device/request path used, the stage reached, and the returned result or blocker.
nonGoals: Do not perform batch onboarding execution.
Do not downgrade a real execution blocker into generic success wording.
Do not widen scope beyond one bounded single-device execution attempt.
acceptance: A durable evidence summary is written for one real single-device execution attempt with a controller-backed trace or exact blocker.
The evidence clearly distinguishes what actually executed, what returned from the controller-backed path, and what remained blocked.
The evidence names the exact action path used and the stage reached, rather than only saying that real execution evidence is missing.
Any non-success, retryable, blocked, or unknown outcome from that execution attempt is preserved explicitly.
notes: Rework target: D2ON-018 stayed on goal but still closed without a real controller-backed single-device execution trace.
Must read first: docs/openclaw-autodev/evidence/d2on/D2ON-018.md and the exact contract/evidence files changed by D2ON-020.
Not enough for DONE: another evidence file that only repeats 'real execution evidence is unavailable' without naming the exact controller-backed stage and blocker.

stories.title: Rework readiness only after the real execution result is high-quality complete
stories.scope: Rework the same readiness carry-forward concern handled by D2ON-019, but update readiness/review only after the new real execution result is high-quality complete for the bounded task. If the controller-backed path still blocks, record that exact blocker, the exact execution stage reached, and the next rework rule instead of closing the batch on a passive wait conclusion.
nonGoals: Do not close the whole program unless the reworked execution result really justifies it.
Do not reintroduce browser-only closure language.
Do not treat a local-only summary as final readiness.
acceptance: final-readiness.md and review.md reflect the reworked real execution result rather than passive waiting on a completed batch.
If a concrete frontend/backend honesty mismatch remains inside allowed paths, it is fixed and validated before the readiness update is finalized.
The next-step note explicitly states whether the real task is now high-quality complete or what exact blocker still requires another execution rework.
The readiness decision must explicitly apply the PRD principle: formal story DONE does not pass unless the real task goal and truthful execution evidence are high-quality complete.
notes: Rework target: D2ON-019 stayed on goal but still ended with a passive blocker note instead of a high-quality completion verdict.
Not enough for DONE: carrying forward another passive wait conclusion without an explicit quality verdict on the real task goal.
